name: Raspberry Cheesecake Brownies
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 350 degrees Fahrenheit.
2. Butter and flour a 13 by 9-inch baking pan.
3. Make brownie batter: In a metal bowl set over a pan of barely simmering water, melt chocolate with butter, stirring until smooth. Cool mixture slightly. Whisk in sugar and eggs, one at a time, then whisk in vanilla and salt. Gradually whisk in flour until just combined. Pour batter evenly into prepared pan.
4. Make cheesecake topping: In a separate bowl, beat cream cheese and sugar until light and fluffy with an electric mixer. Beat in lemon juice, egg, vanilla, and salt. Mix in flour until smooth. Spread topping over the brownie batter.
5. Scatter raspberries over the top and sprinkle with additional sugar.
6. Bake brownies in the middle of the oven for 35 to 40 minutes, or until a tester inserted into the center comes out with crumbs adhering to it. Allow brownies to cool completely in the pan on a rack before serving. Chill at least 6 hours or overnight for best results. Cut into bars and sprinkle with confectioners' sugar before serving.
